snippets: [PubMed 32678530] RECOVERY trial. In hospitalized COVID-19 patients, dexamethasone reduced 28-day mortality (rate ratio 0.83, 95% CI 0.75-0.93). The benefit was seen in patients receiving invasive mechanical ventilation (29.3% vs 41.4%) and those receiving oxygen without ventilation. There was no benefit, and possibly harm, among patients not receiving respiratory support. Dexamethasone dose was 6 mg once daily for up to 10 days.
true: Dexamethasone reduced 28-day mortality in hospitalized COVID-19 patients (RR 0.83, 95% CI 0.75-0.93) [PMID 32678530]
The mortality benefit of dexamethasone was concentrated in patients on invasive mechanical ventilation (29.3% vs 41.4%) [PMID 32678530]
Dexamethasone showed no benefit in COVID-19 patients not receiving respiratory support [PMID 32678530]
planted: Dexamethasone reduced 28-day mortality by 62% in all hospitalized COVID-19 patients regardless of oxygen requirement [PMID 32678530]
The RECOVERY trial found dexamethasone 20 mg twice daily was the optimal dose [PMID 32678530]
Dexamethasone eliminated the need for mechanical ventilation in 80% of ICU patients [PMID 99999999]

snippets: [PubMed 20554319] CRASH-2 trial. Among trauma patients with or at risk of significant bleeding, tranexamic acid reduced all-cause mortality (14.5% vs 16.0%, RR 0.91, 95% CI 0.85-0.97). Risk of death due to bleeding was reduced (4.9% vs 5.7%, RR 0.85). Treatment given within 3 hours of injury was beneficial; treatment after 3 hours appeared to increase the risk of death due to bleeding. There was no increase in vascular occlusive events.
true: Tranexamic acid reduced all-cause mortality in trauma patients (14.5% vs 16.0%, RR 0.91) [PMID 20554319]
Tranexamic acid given after 3 hours of injury appeared to increase risk of death due to bleeding [PMID 20554319]
CRASH-2 found no increase in vascular occlusive events with tranexamic acid [PMID 20554319]
planted: Tranexamic acid reduced mortality most when given 6-12 hours after injury [PMID 20554319]
CRASH-2 showed tranexamic acid tripled the rate of venous thromboembolism [PMID 20554319]
Tranexamic acid reduced all-cause mortality by 45% in the CRASH-2 trial [PMID 20554319]

snippets: [EuropePMC] Systematic review of GenoType MTBDRsl for second-line drug resistance in M. tuberculosis. For fluoroquinolone resistance, pooled sensitivity was 85.1% and specificity 98.2% on indirect testing of culture isolates. For second-line injectable drugs, sensitivity was lower (approximately 76%). Sensitivity for detecting extensively drug-resistant TB (XDR-TB) was modest. The assay does not replace phenotypic drug-susceptibility testing.
true: GenoType MTBDRsl pooled sensitivity for fluoroquinolone resistance was 85.1% with specificity 98.2%
GenoType MTBDRsl sensitivity for second-line injectable drug resistance was lower, around 76%
GenoType MTBDRsl does not replace phenotypic drug-susceptibility testing
planted: GenoType MTBDRsl had 99.9% sensitivity for detecting XDR-TB directly from sputum
The MTBDRsl assay fully replaces the need for culture-based drug-susceptibility testing
GenoType MTBDRsl specificity for fluoroquinolone resistance was only 40%
